<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is likely seeing inconsistent button padding on cards.html.

The cards page contains three button groups: toolbar buttons, Anki export buttons, and filter buttons. The mobile padding override only targets certain selectors, leaving the Anki export buttons without consistent vertical padding. Inline-block display may also be causing vertical alignment issues.

The mobile padding override only covers toolbar and review-start buttons. Anki export buttons retain default `.btn-sm` padding. The user likely means equal top and bottom padding within each button rather than uniform padding across all buttons.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking for a running Hugo dev server and reviewing recent changes.

The Anki export buttons on cards.html appear to lack the mobile padding overrides used by toolbar buttons. Filter chips may also have mismatched padding. Another possibility is that `.btn` elements inherit `leading-relaxed` from the body, which would throw off vertical centering.

The body’s `leading-relaxed` line-height (~1.625) splits unevenly around the text baseline in inline-block buttons, pushing capital letters lower. Switching `.btn` to `inline-flex` with `line-height: 1` would force equal vertical padding. The small-button variant already uses symmetric `py-1.5`.

The Anki export buttons sit outside the toolbar container, so they miss the mobile padding overrides. The exit button uses intentionally asymmetric padding. Review.html needs checking for other button groups.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) &amp;&amp; python3 -c &#34;
import re
css=open(&#39;$CSS&#39;).read()
for m in re.finditer(r&#39;[^@{}]*button\{[^}]+\}&#39;, css):
    s=m.group()
    if &#39;padding&#39; in s and len(s)&lt;500:
        print(s[:400])
        print(&#39;---&#39;)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) && python3 -c "
import re
css=open('$CSS').read()
for m in re.finditer(r'[^@{}]*button\{[^}]+\}', css):
    s=m.group()
    if 'padding' in s and len(s)<500:
        print(s[:400])
        print('---')
"
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) &amp;&amp; python3 -c &#34;
import re
css=open(&#39;$CSS&#39;).read()
# find rules that target button with btn class
for pat in [r&#39;\.btn-sm\{[^}]+\}&#39;, r&#39;\.btn\{[^}]+\}&#39;, r&#39;button\.btn[^}]*\{[^}]+\}&#39;, r&#39;\.btn[^,{]*\{[^}]*padding[^}]+\}&#39;]:
    matches = re.findall(pat, css)
    for m in sorted(set(matches))[:15]:
        print(m[:500])
        print(&#39;---&#39;)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) && python3 -c "
import re
css=open('$CSS').read()
# find rules that target button with btn class
for pat in [r'\.btn-sm\{[^}]+\}', r'\.btn\{[^}]+\}', r'button\.btn[^}]*\{[^}]+\}', r'\.btn[^,{]*\{[^}]*padding[^}]+\}']:
    matches = re.findall(pat, css)
    for m in sorted(set(matches))[:15]:
        print(m[:500])
        print('---')
"
```


_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The uneven vertical padding is caused by a mobile breakpoint at max-width: 1279.98px.

The `.btn-sm` padding is symmetric. The `.btn` class sets `min-height: 2.75rem`. This may cause uneven vertical spacing.

`inline-block` with `min-height` aligns content to the top by default. This makes the bottom appear shorter than the top. Adding `inline-flex items-center` to `.btn` should fix the alignment.

The mobile breakpoint targets many `.btn` selectors with `min-height: 2.75rem`. Only some receive the padding override. This selective application explains why the uneven spacing persists across different buttons.

Regular `.btn` elements retain their default `py-2` padding while receiving `min-height: 2.75rem`. This leaves their text off-center. Toolbar buttons are fully covered by the mobile breakpoint. The fix is to update `.btn` in buttons.css to use flexbox centering.

The fix applies `inline-flex items-center justify-center` to `.btn` with `line-height: 1`. Mobile touch targets should also cover cards page Anki buttons. `.notes-filter` needs the same flex centering since its mobile padding differs from desktop.

`.notes-filter` needs `display: inline-flex` and `align-items: center` to fix its mobile alignment. `.notes-graph-btn` already centers content via flexbox, but its base height of 2rem conflicts with the mobile `min-height: 2.75rem`.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The button padding styles are located in the theme's buttons.css file.

Base `.btn` padding will be fixed in `buttons.css` by adding `inline-flex`, `items-center`, and `justify-center`. Notes-specific buttons will get matching alignment in `custom.css`. The cards page Anki buttons will benefit from the base `.btn` fix too.

The `inline-flex` and `items-center` changes on `.btn` will automatically center content vertically, fixing toolbar and review buttons without extra mobile rules. `.notes-filter` still needs its own fix because it lacks the `.btn` class. `.notes-graph-btn` already centers correctly.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) &amp;&amp; python3 -c &#34;
import re
css=open(&#39;$CSS&#39;).read()
# rules between .btn and button
idx=css.find(&#39;.btn-primary&#39;)
chunk=css[max(0,idx-2000):idx+500]
for m in re.finditer(r&#39;(?:^|[\}.])(button[^\{]*|\.btn[^\{]*)\{[^}]+\}&#39;, chunk):
    print(m.group()[:300])
    print(&#39;---&#39;)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) && python3 -c "
import re
css=open('$CSS').read()
# rules between .btn and button
idx=css.find('.btn-primary')
chunk=css[max(0,idx-2000):idx+500]
for m in re.finditer(r'(?:^|[\}.])(button[^\{]*|\.btn[^\{]*)\{[^}]+\}', chunk):
    print(m.group()[:300])
    print('---')
"
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Adding `text-align: center` to inline-flex elements as well.

The notes-filter uses `display: inline-flex` with `align-items: center`. Mobile padding may be split into `padding-block` and `padding-inline` for clarity. Toolbar buttons grow taller on small screens while Anki buttons stay compact. Desktop buttons already share equal vertical padding.

A broader mobile selector will apply equal padding to all notes-section buttons. The toolbar and Anki export buttons sit in different containers, so the rule must cover both.

The Anki export buttons sit outside the toolbar container, so they missed the mobile padding rules. They lack a unique class, making them hard to target. A shared wrapper would be cleaner, but the current markup needs a different approach.
</details></think>

The issue is `.btn` using `inline-block` with `min-height` on mobile — text sits toward the top instead of centering. Switching to flex centering and aligning filter chips.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com &amp;&amp; npm run build 2&gt;&amp;1 | tail -3 &amp;&amp; CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) &amp;&amp; python3 -c &#34;

&#34; | head -20</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -3 && CSS=$(ls -t /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/css/style.min.*.css | head -1) && python3 -c "
import re
css=open('$CSS').read()
for m in re.findall(r'\.btn\{[^}]+\}', css):
    if 'inline-flex' in m or 'padding-block' in m:
        print(m[:400])
        print('---')
" | head -20
```

```
Total in 10221 ms
[cspHashes] Rewrote 24 inline onerror= attribute(s) in 5 file(s).
[cspHashes] Hashed 4082 inline <script> block(s) → 14 unique hash(es); injected into /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/_headers.
.btn{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;border-radius:.25rem;border-style:var(--tw-border-style);border-width:1px;border-color:transparent;padding-inline:calc(var(--spacing) * 5);padding-block:calc(var(--spacing) * 2);--tw-leading:1;line-height:1;--tw-font-weight:var(--font-weight-semibold);font-weight:var(--font-weight-semibold);text-transform:capitalize;transition-prop
---

```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

Buttons looked uneven because `.btn` used `inline-block` with inherited line-height. On mobile, `min-height: 2.75rem` made the label sit high in the box, so top and bottom padding looked different even when the values matched.

Here’s what changed:

1. **Base `.btn` styles** (`themes/jorap/assets/css/buttons.css`) — switched to `inline-flex items-center justify-center` with `leading-none`, so label text is vertically centered and top/bottom padding stays even.

2. **Filter chips** (`assets/css/custom.css`) — `.notes-filter` and `.notes-graph-filter` use the same flex centering, with explicit `padding-block` / `padding-inline` instead of shorthand.

3. **Cards page Anki panel** — added `notes-action-panel` so those buttons get the same mobile touch-target sizing and symmetric padding as the toolbar on smaller screens.

Rebuild the site (or refresh if the dev server is running) to see the update on `/notes/cards/` and other notes pages.
